route.name: Sardine Lookout Tower Overnighter
route.description: Official/public GPX overnighter with singletrack, doubletrack, gravel roads, and a lookout/camp variant decision.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX overnighter with singletrack, doubletrack, gravel roads, and a lookout/camp variant decision.
route.bestSeason: June to October after snow clears; refresh Tahoe NF closures, smoke, and fire restrictions.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX overnighter with singletrack, doubletrack, gravel roads, and a lookout/camp variant decision.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: No water at the lookout; filter from mapped water sources and carry extra.
resources.food: Carry route food; Loyalton is a detour resupply option.
resources.camping: Sardine Peak Lookout must be reserved or a legal alternate camp confirmed; Bear Valley fallback currently needs ranger confirmation.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote until the Smithneck Road bypass and July 11 camp plan are encoded.
Call Sierraville Ranger District for lookout availability.
Carry more water than the route distance suggests.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Boca-Loyalton Trail / Forest Trail 16E87 closure runs through September 5, 2026 unless Smithneck Road bypass is encoded.
July 11 Sardine Peak Lookout or alternate legal campsite is not confirmed.
Bear Valley fallback is currently marked site closed/non-potable by Tahoe NF sources.
Sardine Lookout Tower Overnighter is not gate-confirmed until the July overnight anchor, water plan, and Tahoe Stage 1 fire-restriction compliance are verified.
Sardine Lookout remains conditional until the lookout or Bear Valley backup is confirmed, Smithneck Road detour is applied, and Tahoe closure/fire checks pass.
Bikepacking route update says Loyalton/Boca Railroad Trail has a washout and riders should stay on Smithneck Road from mile 41.7 to 44.5.
Tahoe National Forest alert lists Forest Trail 16E87 / Boca-Loyalton Trail closed for public health and safety.
July 11 Sardine Peak Lookout booking is not confirmed.
Sardine Peak Lookout has no water; visitors must bring their own supply.
Tahoe National Forest Stage 1 fire restrictions are active for the route season.
Forest Trail 16E87/Boca-Loyalton Trail closure is active through September 5, 2026.
Route guide reports a no-safe-way-around washout and instructs riders to stay on Smithneck Road from mile 41.7 to 44.5, but local GPX is not detoured.
Sardine Peak Lookout exact July 11 booking is not confirmed.
Lookout has no water and Bear Valley fallback water is untreated/not potable.
Tahoe Stage 1 fire restrictions and departure-week smoke/weather remain unresolved.
requiredFixes: Import official GPX plus Smithneck Road bypass.
Call Sierraville Ranger District for July 11 lookout availability.
Refresh Tahoe NF fire restrictions, Boca closure polygon, smoke/weather, and water plan.
Verify Sardine Peak Lookout or an alternate legal campsite for the exact July night.
Confirm all route roads/trails are open and bicycle-legal on current Tahoe National Forest information.
Measure water gaps from the GPX and define a conservative carry plan.
Attach California Campfire Permit/stove compliance notes and refresh Tahoe fire/weather/smoke status within 24-48 hours of departure.
Confirm Sardine Peak Lookout booking or Bear Valley backup plan.
Apply Smithneck Road detour to the GPX or confirm current route legality.
Refresh Tahoe fire restrictions, Boca closures, smoke, and weather within 24-48 hours.
Rework GPX/artifact with the Smithneck Road bypass before promotion.
Confirm Sardine Peak Lookout booking for the exact overnight or verify a legal alternate.
Define water carry/filter plan; do not rely on lookout water.
Add stove/fire compliance notes, including California Campfire Permit if using a stove where allowed.
Refresh Tahoe closures, Stage 1 restrictions, smoke/AQI, and high-elevation forecast on July 8-10.
Create and validate a Smithneck Road bypass GPX/artifact; remeasure mileage, climbing, and water gaps.
Confirm July 11 Sardine Peak Lookout booking by Sierraville Ranger District or verify a legal alternate camp for that exact night.
Build a measured water plan for the detoured route and overnight, assuming no water at the lookout.
Add California Campfire Permit/stove compliance notes for Tahoe Stage 1 restrictions.
Refresh Tahoe alerts, Boca closure pages, AirNow/smoke, and NWS forecast July 8-10.

openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: 70.1 local-GPX miles over 2 ride days is plausible only after detour, camp, water, and fire gates clear.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Local public/output GPX is detailed, but not gate-confirmed because the closure/washout Smithneck Road bypass is not encoded.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Sardine Peak Lookout July 11 booking is not confirmed and Recreation.gov says no water is available; Bear Valley fallback also needs official confirmation.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Tahoe NF Stage 1 fire restrictions are effective June 26 through October 31, 2026; smoke/AQI and forecast must be refreshed July 8-10.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Tahoe NF lists Forest Trail 16E87/Boca-Loyalton Trail closed through September 5, 2026; Bikepacking.com reports a washout from mile 41.7 with Smithneck Road rejoin at mile 44.5.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Lookout has no water; Bear Valley spigot is untreated/not potable; no conservative measured carry/filter plan is finalized.
promotionChecklist.mapArtifactsNote: Terrain/topographic map artifacts are present, but the displayed route line needs the closure detour applied.

readinessEvidence.finding: Tahoe National Forest Stage 1 fire restrictions are effective June 26 through October 31, 2026 and prohibit campfires/stove fires except in listed sites; portable gas/kerosene/jellied/pressurized-fuel stoves with shutoff valves require a valid California Campfire Permit.

readinessEvidence.finding: Tahoe National Forest lists High fire danger on the Eastside and Moderate fire danger on the Westside.
